title: alint configuration (v1)
description: Schema for .alint.yml configuration files. Authoritative reference: docs/design/ARCHITECTURE.md in the alint repository.

version.description: Config schema version. Always 1 for this schema.

extends.description:
Configs to inherit from. Three source kinds are accepted:
  • Local paths (relative to the current file, or absolute): `./base.yml`, `../shared/team.yml`, `/etc/alint/global.yml`.
  • HTTPS URLs with required SHA-256 SRI: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/org/repo/v1/rules.yml#sha256-<64-hex>`. Plain `http://` is rejected; SRI is non-negotiable.
  • Bundled rulesets: `alint://bundled/<name>@<rev>`. Currently shipped: `oss-baseline@v1`, `rust@v1`, `node@v1`, `monorepo@v1`. Offline-resolved (no network, no cache).

Entries resolved left-to-right; later entries override earlier ones, and the current file's own definitions override everything it extends.

**Rule merging is field-level by `id`.** A child can override just the fields it wants to change — `rules: [{id: inherited, level: warning}]` keeps the parent's `kind`/`paths`/etc. and only downgrades severity. `{id: inherited, level: off}` disables a rule without redeclaring it.

Remote and bundled configs cannot themselves declare `extends:` (no principled base for relative resolution in a fetched body); nest extends locally instead.

ignore.description: Additional glob patterns excluded on top of .gitignore.

respect_gitignore.description: Whether to honor .gitignore files during the walk.

vars.description: Free-form string variables referenced from rule messages as {{vars.<name>}} and from `when` clauses as vars.<name>.

facts.description: Properties of the repository evaluated once per run; referenced from `when` clauses as facts.<id>.

rules.description: Rules evaluated against the repository tree.

fix_size_limit.description: Max bytes a content-editing fix will touch. Files over this limit are reported as Skipped with a stderr warning. Default: 1 MiB. Explicit null disables the cap. Path-only fixes (file_create, file_remove, file_rename) ignore this setting.

level.description: Severity. `off` disables a rule (useful when overriding inherited rules).

rule_id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for the rule.

paths_spec.description: A single glob, an array of globs (with optional `!negation`), or an explicit include/exclude pair.

case_convention.description: Case convention. Aliases like PascalCase / pascal / pascal-case / UpperCamelCase all resolve to the same canonical form.

kind.description: The primitive rule kind. See docs/design/ARCHITECTURE.md §4.4 for the full catalog.

message.description: Override the default failure message. Supports `{{vars.*}}` and `{{ctx.*}}` substitution.

policy_url.description: Link to a human-readable policy justification.

when.description: Gate the rule on a fact-based expression (available from v0.2).

fix.description: Automatic-fix strategy applied by `alint fix`. Each rule kind accepts a specific op — alint errors at load time when the op and kind are incompatible.

content.description: Literal bytes to write. Required — pass "" for an empty file.

path.description: Target path, relative to the repo root. When omitted, the first literal entry from the rule's `paths:` list is used.

content.description: Bytes to insert at the start of each violating file. A trailing newline in `content` is the caller's responsibility.
Bytes to append to each violating file. A leading newline in `content` is the caller's responsibility.

file_rename.description: Rename the violating file's stem to the parent rule's target convention (e.g. filename_case's `case:`). Extension is preserved; the file stays in the same directory. No parameters.

file_trim_trailing_whitespace.description: Read the violating file, strip trailing space/tab on every line, write back. Preserves LF or CRLF endings. Skipped on files larger than `fix_size_limit`.

file_append_final_newline.description: Append a single `\n` byte when the file has content but doesn't end with one. No-op on empty files.

file_normalize_line_endings.description: Rewrite every line ending in the file to the parent rule's `target` (lf or crlf). Skipped on files larger than `fix_size_limit`.

file_strip_bidi.description: Remove every Unicode bidi control character (U+202A–202E, U+2066–2069). Skipped on files larger than `fix_size_limit` or on non-UTF-8 content.

file_strip_zero_width.description: Remove every zero-width character (U+200B/C/D, body-internal U+FEFF). A leading BOM is preserved — use `no_bom` to strip that.

file_strip_bom.description: Remove a leading UTF-8 / UTF-16 / UTF-32 BOM from the file. No-op when no BOM is present.

file_collapse_blank_lines.description: Collapse runs of blank lines longer than the parent rule's `max` down to exactly `max` blank lines. Preserves the file's line endings (LF vs CRLF). Skipped on files larger than `fix_size_limit` or on non-UTF-8 content.

rule_kind_dispatch.description: Dispatch on the `kind` discriminator. Each branch sets the required kind-specific fields.

nested_rule.description: A nested rule inside `require:`. Unlike top-level rules, `id` and `level` are synthesized from the parent; the user supplies only `kind` plus kind-specific options.

root_only.description: If true, only files directly at the repository root satisfy the rule.

pattern.description: Rust regex. File contents must match.
Rust regex. File contents must NOT match.
Rust regex. The first `lines` lines of each file in scope must match.

lines.description: Number of leading lines to consider.

max_bytes.description: Maximum allowed file size in bytes.

pattern.description: Rust regex, automatically anchored with ^...$ by the engine.

stem.description: Match the file stem (no extension) instead of the full basename.

rule_pair.description: For every file matching `primary`, a file matching the `partner` template must exist. Template tokens: {dir}, {stem}, {ext}, {basename}, {path}, {parent_name}.

partner.description: Path template resolved per primary match. Example: "{dir}/{stem}.h".

rule_for_each_dir.description: For every directory matching `select`, every rule in `require` must pass. Path-template tokens in the nested rules are pre-substituted per iteration; use `{path}` to refer to the iterated directory itself.

select.description: Glob selecting the directories to iterate.

require.description: Nested rules evaluated against each matched directory.

rule_for_each_file.description: For every file matching `select`, every rule in `require` must pass. Path-template tokens in the nested rules are pre-substituted per iteration.

select.description: Glob selecting the files to iterate.

require.description: Nested rules evaluated against each matched file.

rule_dir_only_contains.description: Every direct child *file* of directories matching `select` must match at least one glob in `allow`. Subdirectories of the matched dir are not checked. Allow globs match the child's basename.

select.description: Glob selecting the directories to enumerate.

allow.description: Basename glob(s) accepted as direct children. Anything else is a violation.

rule_unique_by.description: Flag any group of files (matching `select`) sharing the same rendered `key`. One violation per collision group, anchored on the lexicographically-first file. Path-template tokens ({path}, {dir}, {basename}, {stem}, {ext}, {parent_name}) may appear in `key`.

select.description: Glob selecting the files to deduplicate.

key.description: Path-template producing a key per matched file. Default: {basename}.

rule_dir_contains.description: Every directory matching `select` must have at least one direct child basename matching each glob in `require`. Sugar over for_each_dir + file_exists; use for_each_dir for deeper semantics.

require.description: Basename glob(s) — every dir matching `select` must have at least one child matching each.

rule_every_matching_has.description: Every file OR directory matching `select` must satisfy every nested rule in `require`. Sugar that combines for_each_file + for_each_dir into one rule.

rule_no_trailing_whitespace.description: No line in any file in scope ends with a space or tab. Non-UTF-8 files are skipped silently.

rule_final_newline.description: Every non-empty file in scope must end with a newline byte. Empty files are considered fine.

rule_line_endings.description: Every line ending in every file in scope must use the `target` style (`lf` or `crlf`). Mixed endings within a file also fail.

rule_line_max_width.description: No line in any file in scope exceeds `max_width` Unicode scalar values (chars). Display width is NOT computed — use a formatter for that. Check-only; truncation is not a safe auto-fix.

rule_no_merge_conflict_markers.description: Flag files that still contain git conflict markers at the start of a line (`<<<<<<< `, `=======`, `>>>>>>> `, `||||||| `). Check-only; conflict resolution requires human judgment.

rule_no_bidi_controls.description: Flag Unicode bidirectional control characters (U+202A–202E, U+2066–2069) anywhere in a file. Trojan Source (CVE-2021-42574) defense. Fixable via `file_strip_bidi`.

rule_no_zero_width_chars.description: Flag zero-width characters (U+200B/C/D, plus body-internal U+FEFF). A leading U+FEFF (BOM) is not flagged here — use `no_bom` for that. Fixable via `file_strip_zero_width`.

rule_file_is_ascii.description: Every byte in every file in scope must be < 0x80 (pure ASCII). Stricter than file_is_text; check-only — auto-replacement for non-ASCII bytes would silently lose meaning.

rule_no_bom.description: Flag files that start with a UTF-8 / UTF-16 / UTF-32 BOM. Fixable via `file_strip_bom`.

rule_file_hash.description: Each file in scope must have content whose SHA-256 equals the declared `sha256`. Accepts a bare 64-char hex string or a `sha256:`-prefixed form.

rule_max_directory_depth.description: Every path in scope must sit at depth ≤ `max_depth` (number of `/`-separated components). Check-only; file relocation is a human decision.

rule_max_files_per_directory.description: Every directory containing at least one in-scope file must hold ≤ `max_files` in-scope files (immediate children, non-recursive). Check-only.

rule_no_empty_files.description: Flag zero-byte files. Fixable via `file_remove`, which deletes the empty file.

rule_no_case_conflicts.description: Flag pairs of paths that differ only by case (e.g. README.md + readme.md). These can't coexist on macOS HFS+/APFS or Windows NTFS.

rule_no_illegal_windows_names.description: Reject path components Windows can't represent: reserved device names (CON/PRN/AUX/NUL/COM1-9/LPT1-9 regardless of extension), trailing dots or spaces, and the forbidden chars <>:"|?*.

rule_no_symlinks.description: Flag tracked paths that are symbolic links. Symlinks are a portability headache across Windows/macOS/Linux and CI runners. Fixable via `file_remove`, which deletes the symlink.

rule_executable_bit.description: Assert that every file in scope either has the Unix +x bit set (`require: true`) or does not (`require: false`). No-op on non-Unix platforms. No fix op (chmod auto-apply deferred).

require.description: `true`: +x must be set. `false`: +x must not be set.

rule_executable_has_shebang.description: Every file with the +x bit set must begin with a shebang (`#!`). Catches scripts that were marked executable but whose content is a plain text file or missing a shebang. No-op on non-Unix.

rule_shebang_has_executable.description: Every file that starts with `#!` must have the Unix +x bit set. The inverse of `executable_has_shebang`. No-op on non-Unix. No fix op (chmod auto-apply deferred).

rule_no_submodules.description: Flag the presence of `.gitmodules` at the repo root. Always targets `.gitmodules` — no `paths` field accepted. Fixable via `file_remove`.

rule_indent_style.description: Every non-blank line must indent with the configured style. `tabs` rejects any leading space; `spaces` rejects any leading tab and optionally requires the leading-space count to be a multiple of `width`. Check-only — tab-width-aware reindentation is deferred.

width.description: When `style: spaces`, the leading-space count on every non-blank line must be a multiple of this. Ignored for `style: tabs`.

rule_max_consecutive_blank_lines.description: Cap the number of blank lines that may appear in a row. A blank line is empty or only whitespace. Fixable via `file_collapse_blank_lines`, which collapses over-long runs to exactly `max` blank lines.

max.description: Maximum number of blank lines allowed in a row. `0` means no blank lines at all.

rule_file_starts_with.description: Every file in scope must begin with the given prefix (byte-level). Useful for SPDX headers, generated-file sentinels, magic bytes. Check-only; pair with `file_prepend` if you want auto-prepend (prevents silent duplication on near-matches).

rule_file_ends_with.description: Every file in scope must end with the given suffix (byte-level). For a simple trailing-newline check, prefer `final_newline`. Check-only; pair with `file_append` if you want auto-append.

fact.description: A single fact declaration. Each fact has an `id` plus exactly one kind-specific field.

pattern.description: Rust-regex pattern matched against the content of every file in `paths`. The fact is true iff at least one file contains a match. Non-UTF-8 files are skipped.

git_branch.description: Read `<repo>/.git/HEAD` and return the current branch name (string). Detached HEAD, no `.git/` directory, or any unrecognized layout resolves to an empty string (falsy under `when:`).

argv.description: Program + arguments, passed verbatim to the OS (no shell). Fact value is the process's stdout (trimmed). Non-zero exit, spawn failure, or non-UTF-8 output all resolve to an empty string. SECURITY: `custom:` is only valid in the user's top-level config — alint refuses to load an extended config that declares one.
